note: Added `&& !hasBlockingConfirmed` to the `debate_no_consensus` guard in review_loop.go:408. When one blocking proposal is confirmed and another is insufficient_evidence in the same round, hasBlockingConfirmed=true prevents debate_no_consensus from firing prematurely; acceptance proceeds and the confirmed proposal becomes an open blocker.

Replaced `config.Agents[0]` + `resolveAgentForRole` call in the same-engine fallback with `reviewers[0]` directly (review_critic.go:143). The critic now falls back to the already-resolved first reviewer rather than the first registry entry, which may differ when `pipeline.code_review.by` or `--reviewer` selects a non-first entry.

Added `reviewCriticVerdictMissingVerdict = "missing_verdict"` constant and changed synthetic absent-verdict entries to use that value instead of `reviewCriticVerdictInsufficientEvidence` (review_critic.go:481). The internal handling (counting → unresolved, accept loop → skipped) still works because neither value is `confirmed` or `disputed`.

Same root cause and fix as f_001 — the `debate_no_consensus` guard in review_loop.go:408 now requires `!hasBlockingConfirmed`, so a round with both a confirmed and an insufficient-evidence blocking proposal accepts the confirmed proposal and continues instead of terminating prematurely.
Same root cause and fix as f_002 — the same-engine fallback returns `reviewers[0]` directly, so the critic is the resolved first reviewer regardless of registry order.

Added `allWarnings = append(allWarnings, fmt.Sprintf("critic emitted no verdict for proposal_id %q — treated as missing_verdict", p.ID))` for each synthetic entry (review_critic.go:480), and changed the verdict value to `reviewCriticVerdictMissingVerdict`. Both the wrong artifact value and the missing warning are now corrected.

Removed `omitempty` from `PrecisionCandidates`, `PrecisionConfirmed`, `PrecisionRejected`, and `PrecisionUnresolved` in review_loop.go:107-110. These fields now always appear in JSON output (as 0 when no critic ran); the existing `critic_attempt_id` (still omitempty) distinguishes critic-ran rounds from critic-absent rounds. Updated the exact-schema test in review_loop_test.go to add these four fields to `requiredRound` and `allowedRound`, and also added the optional `critic_*` fields to `allowedRound`.

Updated `renderReviewCriticPrompt` in review_critic.go: the Objective section now describes all three verdicts; the 'How to evaluate' section replaces 'Omit the entry entirely' with 'Set verdict=insufficient_evidence if you cannot determine credibility'; the Rules section lists all three verdict values with per-verdict guidance and removes the 'do not emit a third verdict value' prohibition.

Added `TestReviewRunCriticMixedConfirmedAndInsufficientEvidence` in review_critic_test.go. The reviewer produces two distinct blocking proposals; the critic confirms p_001 and omits p_002 (→ missing_verdict). With `NoFix: true`, the test asserts debate_no_consensus does not fire and the run ends with `findings_open` (confirmed p_001 accepted as blocking).

Added `criticReadOnly []bool` field to `criticTestTransport` and an `allCriticCallsReadOnly()` helper. Added `TestReviewRunCriticReadOnlyExecution` which triggers both an initial and corrective critic call and asserts all recorded `req.ReadOnly` values are `true`.

Replaced the no-op `if ... { _ = got }` block in `TestReviewRunCriticIntraBatchDedupPassesOneToCritic` with a real assertion: the test now reads `runPaths.ReviewLoopSummaryJSON` (capturing the previously discarded third return value of `setupCriticRun`) and asserts `loopSummary.Rounds[0].PrecisionCandidates == 1`.
